parameters.name: delchannels
parameters.label: Delete Channels
parameters.description: Toggle to enable channel deleting.

parameters.name: discard
parameters.label: Delete
parameters.description: Determines whether the scoped channels should be deleted or retained:

parameters.name: select
parameters.label: Select Channels
parameters.description: How to select channels , or By index.

parameters.name: delscope
parameters.label: Channel Names
parameters.description: Enter a scope pattern here to specify the names of channels to delete or extract. You do this by specifying a scope pattern, as detailed in .

parameters.name: selnumbers
parameters.label: Channel Numbers
parameters.description: The indices of the channels to delete or extract. - See possible number patterns below.

parameters.name: chanvalue
parameters.label: Channel Value
parameters.description: Chooses the type of value range selection:

parameters.name: selconst
parameters.label: Select Constant Valued Channels
parameters.description: Select channels which have the same value for all samples. These kinds of channel name patterns are used to select existing channels in an input , see for details.

parameters.name: delsamples
parameters.label: Delete Samples
parameters.description: Toggle to enable sample deleting.

parameters.name: compchans
parameters.label: Channels to Compare
parameters.description: How to select channels used to compare against criteria - By , by index, by using the , or by using the .

parameters.name: compnames
parameters.label: Channel Names
parameters.description: Enter a scope pattern here to specify the names of channels to be used as compare channels. You do this by specifying a scope pattern, as detailed in .

parameters.name: compnums
parameters.label: Channel Numbers
parameters.description: The indices of the channels to be used as compare channels, the default being , the first channel. - See possible number patterns above.

parameters.name: compmulti
parameters.label: Multi-Compare Channels
parameters.description: If there is more that one compare channel, this determines how to treat the values in the compare channels before checking against the criteria:

parameters.name: condition
parameters.label: Delete Condition
parameters.description: Choose the criteria for the samples to be compare against:

parameters.name: value1
parameters.label: Value 1
parameters.description: Set a value for Value 1.

parameters.name: inclvalue1
parameters.label: Include Value 1
parameters.description: Toggle the inclusivity of Value 1.

parameters.name: value2
parameters.label: Value 2
parameters.description: Set a value for Value 2.

parameters.name: inclvalue2
parameters.label: Include Value 2
parameters.description: Toggle the inclusivity of Value 2.

parameters.name: deletecomp
parameters.label: Delete Compare Channels
parameters.description: Determines whether the compare channels should be deleted or retained.

parameters.name: onesample
parameters.label: One Sample if All Deleted
parameters.description: Leaves one sample even when all samples are deleted.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: selrange2
parameters.label: Selrange2
parameters.description: The lower and upper values of the range used for Range Selection.

parameters.name: uniqtol
parameters.label: Uniqtol
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: uniqsamples
parameters.label: Uniqsamples
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: legacypattern
